_comment: The Path content — Dave-owned, editable. Source of truth; regenerate the typed file with `npm run build:path`. Verses are public-domain (KJV/WEB-based); confirm licensing before any modern translation. Glyph ids are placeholders that map to swappable assets in the icon set. Step keys MUST match functions/src/composePath.ts.

intake.q: Where are you two right now?
Where do you hope this is heading?
Which areas do you most want the two of you to talk through?
Does anything feel avoided or unresolved between you?
intake.hint: Pick any that apply. Your partner will never see this.

intake.q: How deep are you ready to go?
Which of these are true for the two of you?
opts: One or both of us has been married before
One or both of us has children
We come from different church traditions
We're at different places in our faith
We come from different cultures or countries
There is a significant age gap between us
One of our families has reservations about us
One or both of us serves in ministry or church leadership
One of us lives with a long-term illness or disability

intake.q: When hard topics come up, what usually happens?
What do you most hope the Path gives the two of you?
opts: Confidence we're right for each other
A stronger walk with God together

steps.name: The Trailhead
steps.verse: Can two walk together, unless they are agreed?
steps.frame: A few gentle openers to find your footing before the road climbs.
steps.atTable: What's one small thing you'd love more of in everyday life together?

steps.name: The Fork
steps.verse: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
steps.frame: Where the road splits, how do the two of you choose a way? This step leans on guessing — see how well you already read each other.
steps.atTable: Name one thing that would help the two of you handle disagreements better.

steps.name: The Storehouse
steps.verse: Honour the Lord with your wealth.
steps.frame: What you keep, what you give, and who decides.
steps.atTable: What's one hope or worry about money you want to put on the table?

steps.name: The Table
steps.verse: Your children will be like olive shoots around your table.
steps.frame: The family you came from, and the one you may build.
steps.atTable: What's one thing about family — the one you came from or the one you might build — you want to talk through?

steps.name: The Garden
steps.verse: A garden enclosed is my sister, my bride.
steps.frame: Tenderness, desire, and what each of you needs to feel loved.
steps.atTable: What's one thing that helps you feel close that you'd like more of?

steps.name: The Valley
steps.verse: Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.
steps.frame: What each of you carries in, and how you will carry it together.
steps.atTable: Is there something from the past you'd like the two of you to understand together?

steps.name: The Hilltop
steps.verse: I will lift up my eyes to the hills. Where does my help come from?
steps.frame: Your walk with God — alone, and side by side.
steps.atTable: What's one thing about your walk with God you'd love to share more of together?

steps.name: The Horizon
steps.verse: For I know the plans I have for you, says the Lord.
steps.frame: Where you each hope the road leads from here.
steps.atTable: What's one dream for the future you want to make sure you've said out loud?

steps.name: The Summit
steps.verse: A wise man built his house on the rock.
steps.frame: The hardest questions, kept for last. No guessing up here — just honest answers, then the reveal.
steps.atTable: What's one conviction you're holding that you want the other to really understand?

steps.name: The Lookout
steps.verse: Your word is a lamp to my feet, and a light to my path.
steps.frame: Turn around. The whole road behind you is lit.

trailhead.v: A man's heart plans his way, but the Lord directs his steps.
Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labour.
Commit your way to the Lord; trust also in him, and he will act.

fork.v: Let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath.
Let not the sun go down upon your wrath.
There is one whose rash words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.

storehouse.v: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance.
Keep your life free from the love of money, and be content with what you have.

table.v: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
Unless the Lord builds the house, they labour in vain who build it.
Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

garden.v: Many waters cannot quench love, neither can the floods drown it.
Your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit. Therefore glorify God in your body.
Love is patient and is kind. Love does not envy, does not boast, is not proud.

valley.v: He heals the broken-hearted, and binds up their wounds.
Do not remember the former things. Behold, I am doing a new thing.
If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.

hilltop.v: As for me and my house, we will serve the Lord.
Let us not give up meeting together, but encourage one another.
Seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things shall be added to you.

horizon.v: Many are the plans in a man's heart, but the counsel of the Lord shall stand.
Where there is no vision, the people perish.
Forgetting the things which are behind, I press on toward the goal.

summit.v: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.
Speaking the truth in love, we will grow up in all things into him.
God gave us not a spirit of fear, but of power, and of love, and of self-control.

lookout.v: Encourage one another and build each other up.
A threefold cord is not quickly broken.
He who began a good work in you will complete it.
